goal: Wire the existing src/scanner/file-watcher.ts (chokidar-based) into a new coderef-watch CLI binary that runs in each consumer workspace, debounces file changes over a 30s window, executes incremental scan + foundation-docs regeneration, emits a heartbeat to .coderef/last-scan.json, and integrates with LLOYD pre-prompt freshness reads. Closes the residual 30-40% staleness gap left by the workorder-Phase-6-only path.

phases.name: WIRE
phases.description: WIRE: convert src/scanner/file-watcher.ts into a coderef-watch CLI bin entry, register in package.json bin map, build dist/src/cli/coderef-watch.js. Verify --help works after npm run build:cli.

tasks.description: Load src/scanner/file-watcher.ts and confirm chokidar API surface
Author src/cli/coderef-watch.ts wrapping FileWatcher with --project-dir and --paths flags
Register coderef-watch in package.json bin map
Build via npm run build:cli and verify dist/src/cli/coderef-watch.js exists
Smoke test: coderef-watch --help prints usage and exits 0
Commit Phase 1: WIRE coderef-watch CLI scaffold

phases.name: DEBOUNCE
phases.description: DEBOUNCE: add 30s debounce window per LLOYD D2 spec. On debounce flush, run incremental coderef-scan + populate-coderef + generate-foundation-docs against changed paths only (not full rebuild).

tasks.description: Confirm LLOYD D2 30s debounce spec from LLOYD-FEEDBACK.md
Add 30s debounce window to FileWatcher event handler in src/cli/coderef-watch.ts
On debounce flush invoke incremental coderef-scan + populate-coderef + generate-foundation-docs against changed-paths set
Synthetic test: edit src/index.ts then wait 31s; verify single regen run, not 2
Commit Phase 2: DEBOUNCE window + incremental regen on flush

phases.name: HEARTBEAT
phases.description: HEARTBEAT: emit .coderef/last-scan.json with {last_scan_at, paths_changed, status, exit_reason}. Atomic temp+rename writes. Schema versioned. Validate JSON against bundled schema before write.

tasks.description: Define heartbeat schema {last_scan_at, paths_changed, status, exit_reason, schema_version} as JSON Schema
On debounce flush write .coderef/last-scan.json via temp+rename atomic pattern
Verify heartbeat is valid JSON after every flush; corrupted-state recovery test
Commit Phase 3: HEARTBEAT atomic writes

phases.name: LLOYD INTEGRATE
phases.description: LLOYD INTEGRATE: document the cheap per-request read pattern (LLOYD computes doc_age_seconds from heartbeat). Emit per-debounce events to LOGS/SESSIONS/{sid}/{domain}/events.jsonl per WO-SESSIONS-EVENT-EMISSION-PROTOCOL-001. Smoke-test from a Python read.

tasks.description: Author docs/LLOYD-INTEGRATION.md describing the LLOYD read pattern (now - last_scan_at = doc_age_seconds)
On debounce flush emit a session event to LOGS/SESSIONS/{sid}/{domain}/events.jsonl per WO-SESSIONS-EVENT-EMISSION-PROTOCOL-001
Smoke-test from a Python read: load .coderef/last-scan.json, compute age, assert < 60s after a fresh edit
Commit Phase 4: LLOYD INTEGRATE + event emission

phases.name: DEPLOYMENT STORY
phases.description: DEPLOYMENT STORY: author docs/DEPLOY-CODEREF-WATCH.md with per-OS service unit instructions (systemd unit for Linux, launchd plist for macOS, Windows Service via nssm or sc.exe, pm2 ecosystem.config.js for Node-managed envs, manual-start fallback). Document explicitly that chokidar runs in CONSUMER WORKSPACE, not LLOYD process, per LLOYD constraint #1.

tasks.description: Author docs/DEPLOY-CODEREF-WATCH.md with per-OS service-unit sections
docs/CLI.md entry for coderef-watch (flags, env, exit codes, where deployment doc lives)
Confirm explicit statement that chokidar runs in CONSUMER WORKSPACE not LLOYD process
Commit Phase 5: DEPLOYMENT STORY

phases.name: CLOSE
phases.description: CLOSE: standard 5-step close (Phase 6 universal gate from prerequisite WO will fire on this WO close - useful smoke test of WO-PHASE-6-CLOSE-GATE-UNIVERSAL-001).

tasks.description: Run /close-workorder against this WO (manual close path)
Verify Phase 6 universal gate (from WO-PHASE-6-CLOSE-GATE-UNIVERSAL-001) fired and regenerated foundation-docs + .coderef/foundation-docs-meta.json
Run validate-docs.js to confirm freshness gate exits 0
Commit Phase 6: WO-CHOKIDAR-DOC-FRESHNESS-DAEMON-001 close
Hard stop: final approval before TRACKING marks completed

plan_type_rationale: Multi-phase, multi-concern: WIRE existing scanner/file-watcher.ts into new CLI bin + ADD debounce + EMIT heartbeat + INTEGRATE with LLOYD pre-prompt + author cross-OS DEPLOYMENT STORY + CLOSE. Prerequisite WO must ship first. Cross-runtime (Node daemon writes file, LLOYD Python reads) + cross-repo (each consumer workspace runs its own daemon) concerns require explicit phase separation.
